title: Padiyanoor Devi Temple
tagline: Padiyanoor Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Padiyanoor Sree Chamundi Devi Temple or Padiyanoor Devi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Chamundi located in Thiruvananthapuram, India.
overview.description: Padiyanoor Sree Chamundi Devi Temple or Padiyanoor Devi Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Chamundi located in Thiruvananthapuram, India. This ancient temple, is situated at Poovachal, around 30 km from Thiruvananthapuram city.

topPlaces.name: Veeranakavu
topPlaces.description: Veeranakavu is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kuttichal
topPlaces.description: Kuttichal (formerly known as Mannoorkara) is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Kuttichal is part of Kattakada Taluk, Vellanad block panchayath and Aruvikkara Legislative Assembly.

topPlaces.name: Kulathummal
topPlaces.description: Kulathummal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kattakada
topPlaces.description: Kattakada is a town in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala. It is situated 13 km north of Neyyattinkara town, 18 km south of Nedumangad town and 20 km east of the Capital City, Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Kootayanimoodu Chamundi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kootayanimoodu sri Chamundeswari temple is a Hindu temple, This ancient temple dates back to the 18th century. The temple is dedicated to Chamundeswari Devi, who is a form of Durga / Shakti.The temple is located in kootayanimoodu, Vellanadu panchayat, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala and is one of the oldest temples in the state.

topPlaces.name: Vellanad
topPlaces.description: Vellanad is a village in the Nedumangad Taluk district of Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mannoorkara
topPlaces.description: Mannoorkara is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Neyyar Dam
topPlaces.description: Neyyar dam is a gravity dam on the Neyyar River in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, South India, located on the foot of the Western Ghats about 30 km from Thiruvananthapuram. It was established in 1958 and is a popular picnic spot.

items.note: Veeranakavu is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

Kuttichal (formerly known as Mannoorkara) is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Kuttichal is part of Kattakada Taluk, Vellanad block panchayath and Aruvikkara Legislative Assembly.
Kulathummal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.
Kattakada is a town in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala. It is situated 13 km north of Neyyattinkara town, 18 km south of Nedumangad town and 20 km east of the Capital City, Thiruvananthapuram.

Kootayanimoodu sri Chamundeswari temple is a Hindu temple, This ancient temple dates back to the 18th century. The temple is dedicated to Chamundeswari Devi, who is a form of Durga / Shakti.The temple is located in kootayanimoodu, Vellanadu panchayat, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala and is one of the oldest temples in the state.
Vellanad is a village in the Nedumangad Taluk district of Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.
Mannoorkara is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

Neyyar dam is a gravity dam on the Neyyar River in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, South India, located on the foot of the Western Ghats about 30 km from Thiruvananthapuram. It was established in 1958 and is a popular picnic spot.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Padiyanoor Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Padiyanoor Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Padiyanoor Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (16 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Padiyanoor Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Padiyanoor Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Padiyanoor Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Padiyanoor Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Padiyanoor Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Veeranakavu, Kuttichal, Kulathummal, Kattakada, Kootayanimoodu Chamundi Temple.
